description:
Your mission is to build a very small “configuration-change tracker” that relies only on the standard
  SQLite CLI (the command called sqlite3) and basic GNU/Linux utilities.

  Target workspace:
    /home/user/cmtracker               – main working directory (must be created)
    /home/user/cmtracker/configs       – will hold three tiny “configuration” files
    /home/user/cmtracker/db            – will hold the SQLite database
    /home/user/cmtracker/reports       – will hold the required text report
    /home/user/cmtracker/logs          – will hold a plain-text log of ALL SQL commands you execute

  Pre-existing data: none.  You must create every directory and every file mentioned below.

  1.  Create the following plain-text configuration files under /home/user/cmtracker/configs with the
      EXACT contents (including the trailing new-line at the end of each file):

        a) /home/user/cmtracker/configs/ssh.cfg
           -------------------------------------
           Port 22
           PermitRootLogin no

        b) /home/user/cmtracker/configs/nginx.conf
           ---------------------------------------
           worker_processes  1;

        c) /home/user/cmtracker/configs/app.ini
           ------------------------------------
           [main]
           debug = true

  2.  Create an SQLite database called /home/user/cmtracker/db/config_changes.db that contains TWO tables.

        Table: files
          id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
          path          TEXT      NOT NULL UNIQUE           -- absolute file path
          checksum      TEXT      NOT NULL                  -- *MD5* of the file’s current contents
          last_modified TEXT      NOT NULL                  -- ISO-8601       e.g. 2023-01-01T12:34:56Z

        Table: changes
          change_id     INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
          file_id       INTEGER   NOT NULL                 -- FK → files.id
          change_type   TEXT      NOT NULL                 -- either ADD or MODIFY
          timestamp     TEXT      NOT NULL                 -- ISO-8601

      Insert an initial “ADD” record for every file so that after this step
        • files contains three rows (one per file)
        • changes contains three rows (one “ADD” per file)

      Use the actual MD5 checksum of each file and the file’s real modification time
      (stat --format %y) converted to the ISO-8601 form “YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ”.
      You may decide how to convert the timezone to UTC; seconds precision is enough.

  3.  Simulate a configuration change:
        • Append the single line “# maintenance window” (without quotes) to
          /home/user/cmtracker/configs/ssh.cfg
        • Update the corresponding row in the files table so that checksum and last_modified
          now reflect the new state of ssh.cfg
        • Add exactly one new row to the changes table noting this as a “MODIFY”.

  4.  Produce a human-readable report that lists every change (i.e. every row in the
      changes table) together with its file path.  The report must be saved to

          /home/user/cmtracker/reports/changes_report.txt

      Output format STRICTLY:

        PATH|TYPE|TIMESTAMP
        /home/user/cmtracker/configs/ssh.cfg|ADD|2023-01-01T…         ← first “ADD”
        /home/user/cmtracker/configs/nginx.conf|ADD|2023-01-01T…
        /home/user/cmtracker/configs/app.ini|ADD|2023-01-01T…
        /home/user/cmtracker/configs/ssh.cfg|MODIFY|2023-01-02T…

      Rules for the report:
        • First line is the header shown above.
        • Every subsequent line is a single change, ordered chronologically by the timestamp column.
        • Use the literal character “|” (pipe) as delimiter with NO surrounding spaces.
        • No extra blank lines at the end of the file.

  5.  Create an SQL command log at

        /home/user/cmtracker/logs/db_commands.log

      The file must contain ONLY the raw SQL commands (one per line) that you executed
      inside sqlite3 in the sequential order you executed them.
      Comments or shell prompts must NOT appear in this file.
      Capitalisation is not important, but commands must be syntactically complete.
      Example excerpt (your exact commands will differ):

        CREATE TABLE files(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, …);
        INSERT INTO files(path, checksum, last_modified) VALUES(…);
        …

  6.  Success criteria the automated checker will enforce:

        a) /home/user/cmtracker/db/config_changes.db exists and is a valid SQLite file.
        b) Schema of the two tables matches EXACTLY (column names, order, types).
        c) files has 3 rows, changes has 4 rows with correct FK relation.
        d) The checksum value stored for every file equals the real, current
           MD5 checksum of that file.
        e) /home/user/cmtracker/reports/changes_report.txt exists and obeys the format,
           header text, column order and delimiter rules above.
        f) The chronological order of lines in the report matches the TIMESTAMP field.
        g) /home/user/cmtracker/logs/db_commands.log exists and contains at least:
             • CREATE TABLE files …;
             • CREATE TABLE changes …;
             • three initial INSERTs into files;
             • three initial INSERTs into changes;
             • one UPDATE to files;
             • one INSERT into changes;
           (The checker only validates presence and order of these canonical statements.)

      Do NOT remove or rename any of the required directories or files.

  Feel free to display intermediate console outputs while you work—only the final
  filesystem state and the required files’ contents will be graded.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth information used by the automated grader.  **DO NOT** reveal these to the agent. -->

  <!-- 1. Exact directory structure that must exist at grading time -->
  <dir>/home/user/cmtracker</dir>
  <dir>/home/user/cmtracker/configs</dir>
  <dir>/home/user/cmtracker/db</dir>
  <dir>/home/user/cmtracker/reports</dir>
  <dir>/home/user/cmtracker/logs</dir>

  <!-- 2. Required configuration file contents BEFORE the simulated change -->
  <file path="/home/user/cmtracker/configs/ssh.cfg" content="Port 22\nPermitRootLogin no\n"/>
  <file path="/home/user/cmtracker/configs/nginx.conf" content="worker_processes  1;\n"/>
  <file path="/home/user/cmtracker/configs/app.ini" content="[main]\ndebug = true\n"/>

  <!-- 3. Required configuration file contents AFTER the simulated change -->
  <file path="/home/user/cmtracker/configs/ssh.cfg" content="Port 22\nPermitRootLogin no\n# maintenance window\n"/>

  <!-- 4. SQLite schema that must be present -->
  <schema db="/home/user/cmtracker/db/config_changes.db">
    CREATE TABLE files (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      checksum TEXT NOT NULL,
      last_modified TEXT NOT NULL
    );
    CREATE TABLE changes (
      change_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      change_type TEXT NOT NULL,
      timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY(file_id) REFERENCES files(id)
    );
  </schema>

  <!-- 5. Relationship expectations -->
  <rowcount table="files" value="3"/>
  <rowcount table="changes" value="4"/>
  <foreignkey source="changes.file_id" target="files.id"/>

  <!-- 6. Report format -->
  <report path="/home/user/cmtracker/reports/changes_report.txt" header="PATH|TYPE|TIMESTAMP"/>

  <!-- 7. Required statements in db_commands.log (order matters, checker allows additional statements between) -->
  <sql_log path="/home/user/cmtracker/logs/db_commands.log">
    CREATE TABLE files
    CREATE TABLE changes
    INSERT INTO files
    INSERT INTO files
    INSERT INTO files
    INSERT INTO changes
    INSERT INTO changes
    INSERT INTO changes
    UPDATE files
    INSERT INTO changes
  </sql_log>
